page: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance within the Cloud GPU family. It features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 1 physical Intel core with 2 threads per core running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU allocation representing a 0.25 slice of the physical GPU, providing 4 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is delivered via an 80 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance split between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated operations. The shared Intel CPU delivers average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1778.19 and average single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth are weak, as evidenced by a passmark CPU score of 3681.75 and a poor database operations score of 1005.99. Conversely, the NVIDIA A16 GPU slice excels at lightweight LLM tasks, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds of 8765.82 tokens per second on a 135M model and 308.92 tokens per second on a 7B model.

This server represents a cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads, offering a fractional hardware profile that avoids the expense of a full dedicated GPU. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation and limited memory bandwidth, which makes it poorly suited for heavy multi-threaded computing, high-throughput database hosting, or large-scale web serving. It is best optimized for entry-level machine learning inference, small-scale LLM prompt processing, and development or testing environments for GPU-enabled applications.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It is equipped with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 16.0 GB of system memory, and an 80 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is provided by a 0.25 slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB of VRAM. While CPU-bound benchmarks and memory bandwidth are relatively weak—as shown by a passmark CPU score of 3681.75—the instance delivers top-tier performance in LLM prompt processing, reaching 308.92 tokens per second on a 7B model. This profile offers a cost-efficient entry point for GPU development, but is poorly suited for database operations or heavy multi-threaded CPU tasks. It is best utilized for lightweight machine learning inference, small model testing, and GPU-accelerated application development.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, an 80 GB NVMe SSD, and a 0.25 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB VRAM, optimized for entry-level machine learning inference.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-16g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 0.25 NVIDIA A16 GPU for lightweight AI inference.
tagline: A cost-efficient fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for entry-level machine learning and development workloads.
bullet_points: 0.25 NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 4 GB VRAM
2 shared Intel vCPUs and 16.0 GB system memory
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs
Best suited for lightweight AI inference and GPU application testing
Weak multi-core CPU and database performance limits heavy backend use
